title: AMC Entertainment Q1 2026 Revenue Report
text: AMC Entertainment, the largest theater chain in the U.S. and globally, reported strong first-quarter financial results driven by improved box office performance. Revenue surged to $1.01 billion, up from $862.5 million year-over-year, marking a 20% increase. Adjusted EBITDA reached positive $38.3 million versus negative $57.7 million previously, representing the best quarterly performance since the pandemic. Total attendance climbed 13% with an average screen count of 9,300. Net losses narrowed to $117 million from $202 million. CEO Adam Aron stated the results are a clear testament to disciplined operating execution in maximizing AMC's revenue growth while simultaneously containing costs. Aron projected continued revenue growth significantly this year, citing box office momentum from films including Project Hail Mary, The Super Mario Galaxy Movie, Michael, and The Devil Wears Prada 2. The theatrical exhibition industry is benefiting from a stronger box office (up nearly 25% in Q1) and studio commitments to maintaining 45-day exclusive theatrical windows. The company has made significant progress toward the path to profitability.
